sections.about: This metric measures the number of trading houses from which purchases are made under the BRSR 'Open-ness of business' disclosure on concentration of purchases. It is a transparency and procurement-concentration indicator, not a Principle 9 consumer Essential Indicator. A lower value indicates better performance because fewer or zero trading-house purchases generally implies more direct procurement and lower intermediary dependence, when read alongside purchases-from-trading-houses percentage.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: In FY2022-23, Tata reported 0 trading houses, JSW reported 25, Jindal reported 636, and SAIL did not report this figure. In FY2023-24, Tata remained at 0, JSW rose to 33, and Jindal reduced to 500; SAIL reported not applicable. In FY2024-25, Tata stayed at 0, JSW reduced to 26, and Jindal further reduced to 478; SAIL remained not applicable. Tata's FY2025 BRSR explicitly reports 0% purchases from trading houses, zero trading houses, and zero top-10 trading-house concentration. SAIL reports Nil purchases from trading houses and Not Applicable for the number of trading houses. Jindal's high count is structurally linked to its stainless value chain: FY2025 purchases from trading houses were 80% of total purchases, from 478 trading houses, with top-10 trading houses accounting for 62% of purchases from trading houses.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata is ranked 1st at zero trading houses. Because the best value is zero, a percentage gap is not mathematically meaningful; on an absolute basis, Tata is 26 trading houses better than JSW and 478 better than Jindal. Tata reported zero trading houses in FY2022-23 and has maintained best-in-class performance in FY2023-24 and FY2024-25. The key positioning message is procurement-route clarity: Tata's openness disclosure indicates no trading-house dependence under the SEBI industry-standard definition, while Jindal's stainless-steel and scrap/alloy-linked sourcing model creates much higher trading-house exposure.
sections.targets: Tata's Responsible Supply Chain Policy and Sustainable Procurement Framework represent relevant procurement commitments. Jindal targets 100% ESG assessment of suppliers.
sections.comparabilityNotes: FY2022-23 values for Tata (0), JSW (25) and Jindal (636) are prior-year comparatives reported in their respective FY2023-24 BRSRs; SAIL did not report this figure for FY2022-23. SAIL reports not applicable for all years on the number of trading houses. This count must be read with purchases from trading houses as a percentage of total purchases: Jindal has both a high number and high purchase share from trading houses.
